content: UHC progress is assessed through the Universal Health Coverage Service Coverage Index (SCI), which tracks 14 tracer indicators across four domains: reproductive/maternal/newborn/child health (family planning, antenatal care, immunization); infectious diseases (HIV, TB, malaria treatment); non-communicable diseases (hypertension treatment, cancer screening); and health system capacity (hospital beds, health workers). Financial protection is measured by incidence of catastrophic health expenditure (>10% household income) and impoverishing health spending. Current global SCI averages 67/100, with wide variation between high-income (80+) and low-income (<50) countries.
